tickets_written.summary: Orphan 'Veteran Studs' chip on discovery filters — 6th chip wraps to centered second row alone
Mini-screener table rows use 1px solid borders instead of 2px dashed — DESIGN.md violation
--ink-light is #6d5a4d but DESIGN.md says #8a7565 — 219 usages wrong, kills typographic hierarchy
Watermark implemented 5 different ways across 15 pages — 3 fonts, 4 opacities, 4 z-indexes
Prompts page has minimal 2-line footer instead of full 6-column nav footer
3 of 6 agent emojis/animals wrong on Situation Room
OG image says 'bloomberg terminal' — old tagline. Should say 'research lab'. Blocks Twitter launch quality.
3 fabricated testimonials with fake Reddit/Twitter usernames. Credibility risk before Reddit seeding.
Logo 'Razzle.lol' in Space Mono — missing font-family: var(--font-display). Brand identity issue.
64 occurrences of 1px solid borders on data rows. DESIGN.md says 2px dashed. Supersedes DES-002.
Pricing cards 12px radius on home, 8px on pricing page. Same component, different pages.
social-card and smart-chip use 3px shadow instead of design-spec 4px.
'60+ panels' on 7 instances across 2 pages. Actual count is 67+. North Star says '70+'.
Demo card says 'The Diplomat' but agent-config.js name is 'Bones'. Brand confusion.
Nav links + buttons use mono font but DESIGN.md type scale says display. Spec/code mismatch needs resolution.
--radius CSS var (12px) defined but barely used. 7+ hardcoded values across home page alone.
btn-primary uses hardcoded color:white — every CTA button lacks dark mode override
Nav active link + Pro/Elite badges use color:white with no dark mode override — navigation broken in dark mode
Auth modal active tab uses color:white — can't tell Login vs Register in dark mode. Conversion path.
Active position chips + command palette badges use color:white/fff — Screener filtering broken in dark mode
Tier list descriptions use hardcoded sand rgba — becomes invisible in dark mode (same color as background)
Gold (#ffd700) and bronze (#cd7f32) medal colors hardcoded in 6 places — no CSS tokens, no dark mode overrides
6+ panel table containers use overflow:hidden — data truncated on mobile instead of horizontal scroll
Pricing cards jump from 3-col to 1-col at 900px — tablets (768-900px) get cramped 3-col layout
Footer grid minmax(140px) too wide for 375px phones — columns don't fit, layout breaks
Home page inline <style> blocks use hardcoded hex colors — bypass dark mode toggle system
Auth modal box-shadow 8px instead of design-spec 4px — only component with oversized shadow
Auth modal border-radius 16px — orphaned value, not a design token (8/12/20px)
Command palette key-label and pos badges use color:#fff with no dark mode override
Plan cards on conversion page have zero :hover CSS — design guide mandates hover-lift
Plan badge (4px) and save badge (6px) border-radius below --radius-sm (8px) minimum
96 instances of color:#fff/white across position/tier/grade badges — should use CSS variable
Diff-mode label uses hardcoded color:white and border-radius:4px — two violations
Compare page PNG export uses hardcoded hex colors instead of getCanvasTheme() — dark mode exports break
Elite CTA button + JS banners use inline styles — fragile, can't be overridden by dark mode/media queries
Nav plan badge + cmd-palette-pos use border-radius:4px — below --radius-sm (8px) design minimum
Footer grid minmax(140px) breaks 375px phones on 71 pages — DES-025 only fixed index.html
border-radius: 10px is not a design token — 50+ instances including logo-mark on every page
border-radius: 16px orphan on 3 pages — not a design token (8/12/20)
box-shadow 6px used at rest on 30+ components — should be 4px base, 6px hover per DESIGN.md
7 elements use border-radius 3px/6px — below design minimum --radius-sm (8px)
121 instances of color:white/#fff in per-page styles — extends DES-032, total 217 sitewide
Bureau connect card has 3 design violations (6px shadow, 16px radius, inline 10px JS cards) — conversion gateway
Promo input (4px) and free-chip (6px) border-radius below --radius-sm (8px) on conversion page
site-footer has no CSS class — all layout via inline styles across 72 files, prevents theming/maintenance
Footer uses <div> instead of <footer> on 67+ pages — no semantic landmark for accessibility
171 instances of border-radius 2-6px below --radius-sm (8px) minimum — affects all Lab panels
16+ sub-minimum border-radius in CSS + JS — Bureau conversion pathway
4 JS badges use color:var(--bg) instead of var(--text-on-accent) — poor light mode contrast
League-card hover shadow = base shadow (no lift). Profile-card hover lift too subtle.
7+ instances of hardcoded hex accent colors in DOM inline styles — should use CSS variables
Heatmap text uses '#fff' instead of var(--text-on-accent) — off-brand in dark mode
Canvas heatmap text uses #ffffff instead of t.white — bypasses dark mode theme
Position tag badges use border-radius:4px — below --radius-sm (8px) minimum
compare-pos-badge and compare-mini-stat use 6px radius — below --radius-sm (8px)
Player cards and stat sections have no :hover state — design guide mandates hover-lift

notes: DES-001 through DES-046 all completed (moved to done/ folder)
DES-047 through DES-056 are open tickets from cycle 5
Cycle 5 theme: JS-GENERATED MARKUP — formula-store.js, lab-panels.js, league-intel.html JS all have design system gaps
lab-panels.css has 171 sub-minimum radius instances — single biggest remaining governance gap (DES-047)
Bureau (league-intel.html) has 3 separate issues: radius (DES-048), badge contrast (DES-049), hover-lift (DES-050)
Canvas export code (charts.js, lab-panels.js) correctly uses getCanvasTheme() for bg/ink — only issue is #fff instead of t.white (DES-053)
warroom.js pixel art hex colors are all acceptable — Situation Room always-dark exception per DESIGN.md
--text-on-accent contrast verified: light mode white-on-orange = 3.1:1 (passes UI component 3:1), dark mode espresso-on-orange = 5.38:1 (passes AA)
about.html panel count correctly says '70+' — DES-013 fix held
No new rogue fonts, no new gradients, no new cold grays, no new blue-black ink — good disciplines still holding
